mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise concurrently, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side resilience, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth exceeds 4.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 900,000 units signals collapsing construction demand and weakening consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts falling below 900,000 units coincides with positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low federal funds rates and declining unemployment creates a favorable liquidity and growth environment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the joint signal predicts negative returns in >55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during normal expansion regimes.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when inflation exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) in high-inflation environments.
